<skill_content name="tdd">
# Skill: tdd

# Test-Driven Development

TDD is the red → green loop. This skill is the reference that makes that loop produce tests worth keeping: what a good test is, where tests go, the anti-patterns, and the rules of the loop. Every section applies on every cycle — consult them before and during the loop, not after.

When exploring the codebase, read `CONTEXT.md` (if it exists) so test names and interface vocabulary match the project's domain language, and respect ADRs in the area you're touching.

## What a good test is

Tests verify behavior through public interfaces, not implementation details. Code can change entirely; tests shouldn't. A good test reads like a specification — "user can checkout with valid cart" tells you exactly what capability exists — and survives refactors because it doesn't care about internal structure.

See [tests.md](tests.md) for examples and [mocking.md](mocking.md) for mocking guidelines.

## Seams — where tests go

A **seam** is the public boundary you test at: the interface where you observe behavior without reaching inside. Tests live at seams, never against internals.

**Test only at pre-agreed seams.** Before writing any test, write down the seams under test and confirm them with the user. No test is written at an unconfirmed seam. You can't test everything — agreeing the seams up front is how testing effort lands on the critical paths and complex logic instead of every edge case.

Ask: "What's the public interface, and which seams should we test?"

## Anti-patterns

- **Implementation-coupled** — mocks internal collaborators, tests private methods, or verifies through a side channel (querying the database instead of using the interface). The tell: the test breaks when you refactor but behavior hasn't changed.
- **Tautological** — the assertion recomputes the expected value the way the code does (`expect(add(a, b)).toBe(a + b)`, a snapshot derived by hand the same way, a constant asserted equal to itself), so it passes by construction and can never disagree with the code. Expected values must come from an independent source of truth — a known-good literal, a worked example, the spec.
- **Horizontal slicing** — writing all tests first, then all implementation. Bulk tests verify _imagined_ behavior: you test the _shape_ of things rather than user-facing behavior, the tests go insensitive to real changes, and you commit to test structure before understanding the implementation. Work in **vertical slices** instead — one test → one implementation → repeat, each test a **tracer bullet** that responds to what the last cycle taught you.

## Rules of the loop

- **Red before green.** Write the failing test first, then only enough code to pass it. Don't anticipate future tests or add speculative features.
- **One slice at a time.** One seam, one test, one minimal implementation per cycle.
- **Refactoring is not part of the loop.** It belongs to the review stage (see the `code-review` skill), not the red → green implementation cycle.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/mseele/.agents/skills/tdd
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.

# Issue tracker: GitHub

Issues and PRDs for this repo live as GitHub issues. Use the `gh` CLI for all operations.

## Conventions

- **Create an issue**: `gh issue create --title "..." --body "..."`. Use a heredoc for multi-line bodies.
- **Read an issue**: `gh issue view <number> --comments`, filtering comments by `jq` and also fetching labels.
- **List issues**: `gh issue list --state open --json number,title,body,labels,comments --jq '[.[] | {number, title, body, labels: [.labels[].name], comments: [.comments[].body]}]'` with appropriate `--label` and `--state` filters.
- **Comment on an issue**: `gh issue comment <number> --body "..."`
- **Apply / remove labels**: `gh issue edit <number> --add-label "..."` / `--remove-label "..."`
- **Close**: `gh issue close <number> --comment "..."`

Infer the repo from `git remote -v` — `gh` does this automatically when run inside a clone.

## Pull requests as a triage surface

**PRs as a request surface: no.** _(Set to `yes` if this repo treats external PRs as feature requests; `/triage` reads this flag.)_

When set to `yes`, PRs run through the same labels and states as issues, using the `gh pr` equivalents:

display.text:
# Issue tracker: GitHub

Issues and PRDs for this repo live as GitHub issues. Use the `gh` CLI for all operations.

## Conventions

- **Create an issue**: `gh issue create --title "..." --body "..."`. Use a heredoc for multi-line bodies.
- **Read an issue**: `gh issue view <number> --comments`, filtering comments by `jq` and also fetching labels.
- **List issues**: `gh issue list --state open --json number,title,body,labels,comments --jq '[.[] | {number, title, body, labels: [.labels[].name], comments: [.comments[].body]}]'` with appropriate `--label` and `--state` filters.
- **Comment on an issue**: `gh issue comment <number> --body "..."`
- **Apply / remove labels**: `gh issue edit <number> --add-label "..."` / `--remove-label "..."`
- **Close**: `gh issue close <number> --comment "..."`

Infer the repo from `git remote -v` — `gh` does this automatically when run inside a clone.

## Pull requests as a triage surface

**PRs as a request surface: no.** _(Set to `yes` if this repo treats external PRs as feature requests; `/triage` reads this flag.)_

When set to `yes`, PRs run through the same labels and states as issues, using the `gh pr` equivalents:

- **Read a PR**: `gh pr view <number> --comments` and `gh pr diff <number>` for the diff.
- **List external PRs for triage**: `gh pr list --state open --json number,title,body,labels,author,authorAssociation,comments` then keep only `authorAssociation` of `CONTRIBUTOR`, `FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR`, or `NONE` (drop `OWNER`/`MEMBER`/`COLLABORATOR`).
- **Comment / label / close**: `gh pr comment`, `gh pr edit --add-label`/`--remove-label`, `gh pr close`.

GitHub shares one number space across issues and PRs, so a bare `#42` may be either — resolve with `gh pr view 42` and fall back to `gh issue view 42`.

## When a skill says "publish to the issue tracker"

Create a GitHub issue.

## When a skill says "fetch the relevant ticket"

Run `gh issue view <number> --comments`.

Goal: Convert `src/logic/events.rs` (2,351 LOC, 18 public functions) into directory module. First slice: extract notification fan-out — the ~4 functions that build notification emails and the templates they use.

Target:
```
src/logic/events/
  mod.rs           # pub use siblings; the remaining ~14 fns
  notifications.rs # the 4 notification fns + their templates
```

Rules:
- Only notification functions move (4 of them). Booking lifecycle, payment operations, event-query functions stay.
- `events::notifications` takes `&impl EmailGateway` directly (the seam from #71) and stops being a sibling of the rest of `events.rs` for email access.
- `api.rs` call sites for moved functions update to `events::notifications::*`.
- Notification tests move next to `notifications.rs` and run offline via `EmailGateway` mock from #71.

- Exactly 4 notification functions move; no other code moves
- `events::notifications` accepts `&impl EmailGateway` and doesn't reach across to rest of events.rs for email access
- Notification tests live next to `notifications.rs` and run fully offline via EmailGateway mock
- No behavior change in production paths
- `cargo test` passes
- `cargo clippy --all-features` is clean

134:         for (booking, _, _) in bookings {

136:                 template::render_schedule_change(template, &booking, &event, &removed_dates)?;
137: 
138:             messages.push(
139:                 Email::new(message_type, booking.email, subject.clone(), body, None)
140:                     .into_message(&email_account, email_sender)?,
141:             );
142:         }
143: 
144:         email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
145:     }
146:     Ok(event)
147: }

153: pub(crate) async fn verify_payments(
154:     pool: &PgPool,
155:     csv: String,
156:     csv_start_date: Option<NaiveDate>,
157: ) -> Result<Vec<VerifyPaymentResult>> {
158:     let bytes = STANDARD
159:         .decode(&csv)
160:         .with_context(|| format!("Error decoding the cvs content: {}", csv))?;
161:     let csv = match ISO_8859_1.decode(&bytes, DecoderTrap::Strict) {
162:         Ok(value) => value,
163:         Err(e) => bail!("Decoding csv content with ISO 8859: {}", e.into_owned()),
164:     };
165: 
166:     let payment_records =
167:         tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || read_payment_records(&csv, csv_start_date)).await??;
168:     let payment_ids = payment_records
169:         .iter()
170:         .flat_map(|r| &r.payment_ids)
171:         .collect::<HashSet<_>>();
172:     let mut bookings = db::get_bookings_to_verify_payment(pool, payment_ids).await?;
173:     let (verified_payments, result) =
174:         compare_payment_records_with_bookings(&payment_records, &mut bookings)?;
175:     if !verified_payments.is_empty() {
176:         db::mark_as_paid(pool, &verified_payments).await?;
177:     }
178: 
179:     Ok(result)
180: }

182: pub(crate) async fn get_unpaid_bookings(
183:     pool: &PgPool,
184:     event_type: EventType,
185: ) -> Result<Vec<UnpaidEventBooking>> {
186:     let result = db::get_event_bookings_without_payment(pool, event_type).await?;
187: 
188:     let mut bookings = Vec::new();
189: 
190:     for (mut booking, booking_insert_date, first_event_date, event_template) in result.into_iter() {
191:         if let Some(first_event_date) = first_event_date {
192:             let booking_date;
193:             if let Some(payment_reminder_sent) = booking.payment_reminder_sent {
194:                 booking_date = payment_reminder_sent
195:                     + Duration::try_days(3).with_context(|| "Cannot create duration of 3 days.")?;
196:             } else {
197:                 booking_date = booking_insert_date;
198:             }
199:             let due_in_days = calc_due_in_days(booking_date, first_event_date, event_template)?;
200:             booking.due_in_days = Some(due_in_days);
201:         }
202:         bookings.push(booking);
203:     }
204: 
205:     Ok(bookings)
206: }
207: 
208: /// calculate the days until the booking should be paid
209: fn calc_due_in_days(
210:     booking_date: DateTime<Utc>,
211:     first_event_date: DateTime<Utc>,
212:     event_template: String,
213: ) -> Result<i64> {
214:     // initialize regex
215:     lazy_static! {
216:         static ref PAYDAY_REGEX: Regex =
217:             Regex::new(r"\{\{payday\s+(\d+)\}\}").expect("regex is valid");
218:     }
219: 
220:     // calculate custom days - if defined in the event template
221:     let custom_days;
222:     if let Some(captures) = PAYDAY_REGEX.captures(&event_template) {
223:         custom_days = Some(
224:             captures
225:                 .get(1)
226:                 .map_or("", |m| m.as_str())
227:                 .parse::<i64>()
228:                 .expect("custom day is an integer"),
229:         );
230:     } else {
231:         custom_days = None;
232:     }
233: 
234:     // get the payday for the event
235:     let payday = calculate_payday(&booking_date, &first_event_date, custom_days)?;
236: 
237:     // calculate due in days
238:     let due_in_days = payday - Utc::now().date_naive();
239: 
240:     Ok(due_in_days.num_days())
241: }

251: pub(crate) async fn export_sepa_xml(
252:     pool: &PgPool,
253:     event_id: EventId,
254:     secrets: &dyn SecretProvider,
255: ) -> Result<(String, String)> {
256:     use crate::models::SepaExportError;
257: 
258:     let event = db::get_event(pool, &event_id, false)
259:         .await?
260:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event not found"))?;
261: 
262:     if event.payment_method != PaymentMethod::SepaDirectDebit {
263:         return Err(anyhow::Error::from(SepaExportError::NotASepaEvent));
264:     }
265: 
266:     let creditor_name = secrets.get(SecretKey::SepaCreditorName).await?;
267:     let creditor_iban = secrets.get(SecretKey::SepaCreditorIban).await?;
268:     let creditor_id = secrets.get(SecretKey::SepaCreditorId).await?;
269: 
270:     if creditor_name.is_empty() || creditor_iban.is_empty() || creditor_id.is_empty() {
271:         return Err(anyhow::Error::from(SepaExportError::ConfigIncomplete));
272:     }
273:     let creditor_bic = banking::lookup_bic(&creditor_iban)
274:         .await
275:         .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to lookup creditor BIC: {}", e))?;
276: 
277:     let mut tx = pool.begin().await?;
278: 
279:     db::lock_sepa_eligible_bookings(&mut tx, event_id).await?;
280: 
281:     let bookings = db::get_sepa_eligible_bookings(&mut tx, event_id).await?;
282: 
283:     if bookings.is_empty() {
284:         return Err(anyhow::Error::from(SepaExportError::NoBookingsAvailable));
285:     }
286: 
287:     let mut booking_data = Vec::new();
288:     let mut failed_ibans = Vec::new();
289:     for sub in &bookings {
290:         match sub.iban.as_ref() {
291:             Some(iban) => match banking::lookup_bic(iban).await {
292:                 Ok(bic) => booking_data.push((sub.clone(), bic)),
293:                 Err(_) => failed_ibans.push(iban.clone()),
294:             },
295:             None => failed_ibans.push("(missing)".to_string()),
296:         }
297:     }
298: 
299:     if !failed_ibans.is_empty() {
300:         return Err(anyhow::Error::from(SepaExportError::BicLookupFailed(
301:             format!("BIC lookup failed for IBAN(s): {}", failed_ibans.join(", ")),
302:         )));
303:     }
304: 
305:     let xml = banking::generate_sepa_xml(
306:         &event,
307:         &booking_data,
308:         &creditor_name,
309:         &creditor_iban,
310:         &creditor_bic,
311:         &creditor_id,
312:     )?;
313: 
314:     let booking_ids: Vec<i32> = bookings.iter().map(|b| b.id).collect();
315:     db::mark_sepa_exported(&mut tx, &booking_ids).await?;
316: 
317:     tx.commit().await?;
318: 
319:     let filename = format!(
320:         "sepa-{}-{}.xml",
321:         event.name.replace(' ', "_").to_lowercase(),
322:         Utc::now().format("%Y-%m-%d"),
323:     );
324: 
325:     Ok((filename, xml))
326: }

328: pub(crate) async fn cancel_booking(
329:     pool: &PgPool,
330:     booking_id: i32,
331:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
332: ) -> Result<()> {
333:     let (event, canceled_booking, waiting_list_booking) =
334:         db::cancel_event_booking(pool, booking_id).await?;
335: 
336:     let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
337:     let mut messages = Vec::new();
338: 
339:     // create cancellation confirmation email
340:     let subject = format!("{} Stornierung Buchung", event.subject_prefix());
341:     let body = match event.event_type {
342:         EventType::Fitness => include_str!("../../templates/cancel_booking_fitness.txt"),
343:         EventType::Events => include_str!("../../templates/cancel_booking_events.txt"),
344:     };
345:     let body = template::render_booking(body, &canceled_booking, &event, None, None, None)?;
346:     messages.push(
347:         email_sender
348:             .build_message(&email_account)?
349:             .to(canceled_booking.email.parse()?)
350:             .bcc(email_account.mailbox()?)
351:             .subject(subject)
352:             .singlepart(SinglePart::plain(body))?,
353:     );
354: 
355:     // create booking confirmation email for the new booking
356:     if let Some((new_booking, payment_id)) = waiting_list_booking {
357:         let subject = format!("{} Bestätigung Buchung", event.subject_prefix());
358:         let body = template::render_booking(
359:             &event.booking_template,
360:             &new_booking,
361:             &event,
362:             Some(payment_id),
363:             None,
364:             Some(false),
365:         )?;
366: 
367:         messages.push(
368:             email_sender
369:                 .build_message(&email_account)?
370:                 .to(new_booking.email.parse()?)
371:                 .bcc(email_account.mailbox()?)
372:                 .subject(subject)
373:                 .singlepart(SinglePart::plain(body))?,
374:         );
375:     }
376: 
377:     email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
378: 
379:     Ok(())
380: }

382: pub(crate) async fn send_event_email(
383:     pool: &PgPool,
384:     data: EventEmail,
385:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
386: ) -> Result<()> {
387:     if !data.bookings && !data.waiting_list {
388:         bail!("Either bookings or waiting list option need to be selected to send an event email.")
389:     }
390:     let enrolled = if data.bookings && !data.waiting_list {
391:         Some(true)
392:     } else if data.waiting_list && !data.bookings {
393:         Some(false)
394:     } else {
395:         None
396:     };
397: 
398:     let bookings = db::get_bookings(pool, &data.event_id, enrolled).await?;
399:     if bookings.is_empty() {
400:         return Ok(());
401:     }
402: 
403:     // calculate correct event id
404:     let event_id = match &data.prebooking_event_id {
405:         Some(event_id) => event_id,
406:         None => &data.event_id,
407:     };
408: 
409:     let event = db::get_event(pool, event_id, false)
410:         .await?
411:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Found no event with id '{}'", event_id))?;
412: 
413:     let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
414:     let message_type: MessageType = event.event_type.into();
415:     let mut messages = Vec::new();
416: 
417:     for (booking, subscriber_id, payment_id) in bookings {
418:         let prebooking_link;
419:         if let Some(event_id) = data.prebooking_event_id {
420:             prebooking_link = Some(create_prebooking_link(
421:                 event.event_type,
422:                 event_id,
423:                 subscriber_id,
424:             )?);
425:         } else {
426:             prebooking_link = None;
427:         }
428: 
429:         let body = template::render_booking(
430:             &data.body,
431:             &booking,
432:             &event,
433:             Some(payment_id),
434:             prebooking_link,
435:             None,
436:         )?;
437: 
438:         let attachments = data
439:             .attachments
440:             .as_ref()
441:             .map(|attachments| attachments.to_vec());
442: 
443:         messages.push(
444:             Email::new(
445:                 message_type,
446:                 booking.email,
447:                 data.subject.clone(),
448:                 body,
449:                 attachments,
450:             )
451:             .into_message(&email_account, email_sender)?,
452:         );
453:     }
454: 
455:     email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
456: 
457:     Ok(())
458: }

460: /// send a reminder email for each events that starts next week
461: pub(crate) async fn send_event_reminders(
462:     pool: &PgPool,
463:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
464: ) -> Result<usize> {
465:     // get all events where a event reminder should be send to the subscribers
466:     let events = db::get_reminder_events(pool).await?;
467: 
468:     // process each event
469:     for event in &events {
470:         // prepare for message generation
471:         let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
472:         let message_type: MessageType = event.event_type.into();
473:         let mut messages = Vec::new();
474: 
475:         // get the subject and body (depending on the event type)
476:         let (subject, body) = match event.event_type {
477:             EventType::Fitness => (
478:                 format!("{} Info zum Kursstart", event.subject_prefix()),
479:                 include_str!("../../templates/event_reminder_fitness.txt"),
480:             ),
481:             EventType::Events => (
482:                 format!("{} Info zum Eventstart", event.subject_prefix()),
483:                 include_str!("../../templates/event_reminder_events.txt"),
484:             ),
485:         };
486: 
487:         // iterate all enrolled event subscribers (Option should never be None)
488:         if let Some(subscribers) = &event.subscribers {
489:             for subscriber in subscribers.iter().filter(|s| s.enrolled) {
490:                 // render the body for the email...
491:                 let body = template::render_event_reminder(body, event, subscriber)?;
492: 
493:                 // ...and push the email into the messages list
494:                 messages.push(
495:                     Email::new(
496:                         message_type,
497:                         subscriber.email.clone(),
498:                         subject.clone(),
499:                         body,
500:                         None,
501:                     )
502:                     .into_message(&email_account, email_sender)?,
503:                 );
504:             }
505: 
506:             // send reminder emails to all event subribers
507:             email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
508: 
509:             // mark reminder has been sent to the event
510:             // (to avoid duplicate sending of reminder emails)
511:             db::mark_as_reminder_sent(pool, &event.id).await?;
512:         }
513:     }
514: 
515:     // return the count of events for which the reminder has been send
516:     Ok(events.len())
517: }

519: /// send a reminder email for all bookings which are due with payment
520: pub(crate) async fn send_payment_reminders(
521:     pool: &PgPool,
522:     event_type: EventType,
523:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
524: ) -> Result<usize> {
525:     // get unpaid bookings and filter for bookings that are due with payment
526:     let bookings = get_unpaid_bookings(pool, event_type)
527:         .await?
528:         .into_iter()
529:         .filter(|booking| matches!(booking.due_in_days, Some(due_in_days) if due_in_days < 0))
530:         .collect::<Vec<_>>();
531: 
532:     // prepare for message generation
533:     let email_account = email_sender
534:         .account(AccountLookup::ByType(event_type.into()))
535:         .await?;
536:     let message_type: MessageType = event_type.into();
537:     let mut messages = Vec::new();
538: 
539:     // get the subject and body (depending on the event type)
540:     let subject = format!("{} Zahlungserinnerung", event_type.subject_prefix());
541:     let body = match event_type {
542:         EventType::Fitness => include_str!("../../templates/payment_reminder_fitness.txt"),
543:         EventType::Events => include_str!("../../templates/payment_reminder_events.txt"),
544:     };
545: 
546:     let mut event_cache = HashMap::new();
547:     for booking in bookings.iter() {
548:         // get the event from the cache of from the database
549:         let key = booking.event_id;
550:         if let Entry::Vacant(e) = event_cache.entry(key) {
551:             let value = db::get_event(pool, &booking.event_id, false)
552:                 .await?
553:                 .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event with id '{}' is missing", key))?;
554:             e.insert(value);
555:         }
556:         let event = event_cache
557:             .get(&key)
558:             .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event with id '{}' is not in the cache", key))?;
559: 
560:         // render the body for the email...
561:         let body = template::render_payment_reminder(body, event, booking)?;
562: 
563:         // ...and push the email into the messages list
564:         messages.push(
565:             Email::new(
566:                 message_type,
567:                 booking.email.clone(),
568:                 subject.clone(),
569:                 body,
570:                 None,
571:             )
572:             .into_message(&email_account, email_sender)?,
573:         );
574:     }
575: 
576:     // send reminder emails to all bookings due with payment
577:     email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
578: 
579:     // mark payment reminder has been sent to the bookings
580:     // (to avoid duplicate sending of reminder emails)
581:     let booking_ids = bookings
582:         .into_iter()
583:         .map(|booking| booking.booking_id)
584:         .collect::<Vec<_>>();
585:     db::mark_as_payment_reminder_sent(pool, &booking_ids).await?;
586: 
587:     Ok(booking_ids.len())
588: }

590: /// Check that an event has finished. If so, move the event to status 'Finished',
591: /// send the attendee confirmation email, and move the event to status 'Closed'.
592: pub(crate) async fn close_finished_running_events(
593:     pool: &PgPool,
594:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
595: ) -> Result<usize> {
596:     let mut count = 0;
597: 
598:     for event_id in db::get_all_finished_event_ids(pool).await? {
599:         // move event into status finsihed
600:         let event = update(
601:             pool,
602:             PartialEvent {
603:                 id: Some(event_id),
604:                 lifecycle_status: Some(LifecycleStatus::Finished),
605:                 ..Default::default()
606:             },
607:             email_sender,
608:         )
609:         .await?;
610: 
611:         // send confirmation emails for fitness events
612:         if matches!(event.event_type, EventType::Fitness) {
613:             send_participation_confirmation(pool, event_id, email_sender).await?;
614:         }
615: 
616:         // move event into status closed
617:         update(
618:             pool,
619:             PartialEvent {
620:                 id: Some(event_id),
621:                 lifecycle_status: Some(LifecycleStatus::Closed),
622:                 ..Default::default()
623:             },
624:             email_sender,
625:         )
626:         .await?;
627: 
628:         count += 1;
629:     }
630: 
631:     Ok(count)
632: }

642: async fn book_event(
643:     pool: &PgPool,
644:     mut booking: EventBooking,
645:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
646: ) -> Result<BookingResponse> {
647:     let event = db::get_event(pool, &booking.event_id, false)
648:         .await?
649:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event not found"))?;
650: 
651:     if event.payment_method == PaymentMethod::SepaDirectDebit {
652:         let raw_iban = booking.iban.as_ref().ok_or_else(|| {
653:             warn!(
654:                 "IBAN missing for SEPA booking of event {}",
655:                 booking.event_id
656:             );
657:             ValidationError::new("Bitte gib eine gültige IBAN ein.")
658:         })?;
659:         let normalized = banking::validate_iban_str(raw_iban)?;
660:         booking.iban = Some(normalized);
661:     } else {
662:         booking.iban = None;
663:     }
664: 
665:     let price_multiplier = event.price_relevant_multiplier(&booking.custom_values);
666:     if event.custom_fields.iter().any(|cf| cf.price_relevant) && price_multiplier.is_none() {
667:         bail!(ValidationError::new(
668:             "Bitte gib eine gültige Anzahl ein.".to_string()
669:         ));
670:     }
671: 
672:     let booking_result = db::book_event(pool, &booking).await?;
673:     let booking_response = match booking_result {
674:         BookingResult::Booked(event, counter, payment_id) => {
675:             process_booking(
676:                 pool,
677:                 &booking,
678:                 event,
679:                 counter,
680:                 true,
681:                 payment_id,
682:                 email_sender,
683:             )
684:             .await?
685:         }
686:         BookingResult::WaitingList(event, counter, payment_id) => {
687:             process_booking(
688:                 pool,
689:                 &booking,
690:                 event,
691:                 counter,
692:                 false,
693:                 payment_id,
694:                 email_sender,
695:             )
696:             .await?
697:         }
698:         BookingResult::DuplicateBooking => {
699:             info!(
700:                 "Event ({}) booking failed because a duplicate booking has been detected.",
701:                 booking.event_id
702:             );
703:             BookingResponse::failure(
704:                 "Wir haben schon eine Buchung mit diesen Anmeldedaten erkannt. Bitte verwende für weitere Buchungen andere Anmeldedaten.",
705:             )
706:         }
707:         BookingResult::NotBookable => {
708:             info!(
709:                 "Event ({}) booking failed because the event is in a unbookable state.",
710:                 booking.event_id
711:             );
712:             BookingResponse::failure("Das Event kann aktuell leider nicht gebucht werden.")
713:         }
714:         BookingResult::BookedOut => {
715:             info!(
716:                 "Booking failed because Event ({}) was overbooked.",
717:                 booking.event_id
718:             );
719:             BookingResponse::failure(MESSAGE_FAIL)
720:         }
721:     };
722: 
723:     Ok(booking_response)
724: }
725: 
726: async fn pre_book_event(
727:     pool: &PgPool,
728:     hash: String,
729:     provided_iban: Option<String>,
730:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
731: ) -> Result<BookingResponse> {
732:     let ids = hashids::decode(&hash)
733:         .with_context(|| format!("Error decoding the prebooking hash {} into ids", hash))?;
734: 
735:     if ids.len() != 2 {
736:         bail!(
737:             "Booking failed because decoded prebooking hash ({}) has an invalid length: {}",
738:             hash,
739:             ids.len()
740:         );
741:     }
742: 
743:     let event_id = EventId::from(i32::try_from(ids[0])?);
744:     let subscriber_id: i32 = ids[1].try_into()?;
745: 
746:     let event = db::get_event(pool, &event_id, false)
747:         .await?
748:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event not found"))?;
749: 
750:     if !event.lifecycle_status.is_bookable() {
751:         return Ok(BookingResponse::failure(
752:             "Diese Veranstaltung ist aktuell nicht buchbar.",
753:         ));
754:     }
755: 
756:     let mut iban = provided_iban;
757:     if event.payment_method == PaymentMethod::SepaDirectDebit && iban.is_none() {
758:         let prior_iban = db::find_prior_sepa_iban(pool, subscriber_id).await?;
759:         if let Some(prior) = prior_iban {
760:             iban = Some(prior);
761:         } else {
762:             return Ok(BookingResponse::requires_iban("Bitte gib deine IBAN ein."));
763:         }
764:     }
765: 
766:     let (booking_result, booking) = db::pre_book_event(pool, event_id, subscriber_id, iban).await?;
767:     let booking_response = match booking_result {
768:         BookingResult::Booked(event, counter, payment_id) => {
769:             process_booking(
770:                 pool,
771:                 &booking
772:                     .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Found no 'virtual' booking. Never should be here."))?,
773:                 event,
774:                 counter,
775:                 true,
776:                 payment_id,
777:                 email_sender,
778:             )
779:             .await?
780:         }
781:         BookingResult::WaitingList(event, counter, payment_id) => {
782:             process_booking(
783:                 pool,
784:                 &booking
785:                     .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Found no 'virtual' booking. Never should be here."))?,
786:                 event,
787:                 counter,
788:                 false,
789:                 payment_id,
790:                 email_sender,
791:             )
792:             .await?
793:         }
794:         BookingResult::DuplicateBooking => {
795:             warn!(
796:                 "Prebooking link data has been detected and invalidated for booking {:?}",
797:                 booking
798:             );
799:             BookingResponse::failure("Der Buchungslink wurde schon benutzt und ist daher ungültig.")
800:         }
801:         BookingResult::NotBookable => {
802:             warn!(
803:                 "Prebooking is not possible because the event is in a unbookable state for booking {:?}",
804:                 booking
805:             );
806:             BookingResponse::failure("Das Event kann aktuell leider nicht gebucht werden.")
807:         }
808:         BookingResult::BookedOut => {
809:             BookingResponse::failure("Das Event ist leider schon ausgebucht.")
810:         }
811:     };
812: 
813:     Ok(booking_response)
814: }
815: 
816: async fn process_booking(
817:     pool: &PgPool,
818:     booking: &EventBooking,
819:     event: Event,
820:     counter: Vec<EventCounter>,
821:     booked: bool,
822:     payment_id: String,
823:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
824: ) -> Result<BookingResponse> {
825:     subscribe_to_updates(pool, booking, &event, email_sender).await?;
826:     send_booking_mail(booking, &event, booked, payment_id, email_sender).await?;
827:     info!("Booking of Event {} was successfull", booking.event_id);
828:     let message = if booked {
829:         "Die Buchung war erfolgreich. Du bekommst in den nächsten Minuten eine Bestätigung per E-Mail."
830:     } else {
831:         "Du stehst jetzt auf der Warteliste. Wir benachrichtigen Dich, wenn Plätze frei werden."
832:     };
833:     Ok(BookingResponse::success(message, counter))
834: }
835: 
836: async fn subscribe_to_updates(
837:     pool: &PgPool,
838:     booking: &EventBooking,
839:     event: &Event,
840:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
841: ) -> Result<()> {
842:     // only subscribe to updates if updates field is true
843:     if !booking.updates.unwrap_or(false) {
844:         return Ok(());
845:     }
846:     let subscription = NewsSubscription::new(booking.email.clone(), vec![event.event_type.into()]);
847:     super::news::subscribe_to_news(pool, subscription, false, email_sender).await?;
848: 
849:     Ok(())
850: }
851: 
852: async fn send_booking_mail(
853:     booking: &EventBooking,
854:     event: &Event,
855:     booked: bool,
856:     payment_id: String,
857:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
858: ) -> Result<()> {
859:     let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
860:     let subject;
861:     let template: &str;
862:     let opt_payment_id;
863:     if booked {
864:         subject = format!("{} Bestätigung Buchung", event.subject_prefix());
865:         template = &event.booking_template;
866:         opt_payment_id = Some(payment_id);
867:     } else {
868:         subject = format!("{} Bestätigung Warteliste", event.subject_prefix());
869:         template = match event.event_type {
870:             EventType::Fitness => include_str!("../../templates/waiting_list_fitness.txt"),
871:             EventType::Events => include_str!("../../templates/waiting_list_events.txt"),
872:         };
873:         opt_payment_id = None;
874:     }
875: 
876:     let mut body =
877:         template::render_booking(template, booking, event, opt_payment_id, None, Some(true))?;
878: 
879:     if booking.updates.unwrap_or(false) {
880:         body.push_str(
881:             format!(
882:                 "
883: 
884: PS: Ab sofort erhältst Du automatisch eine E-Mail, sobald neue {} online sind.
885: {}",
886:                 match event.event_type {
887:                     EventType::Fitness => "Kursangebote",
888:                     EventType::Events => "Events",
889:                 },
890:                 super::news::UNSUBSCRIBE_MESSAGE
891:             )
892:             .as_str(),
893:         )
894:     }
895: 
896:     let message = email_sender
897:         .build_message(&email_account)?
898:         .to(booking.email.parse()?)
899:         .bcc(email_account.mailbox()?)
900:         .subject(subject)
901:         .singlepart(SinglePart::plain(body))?;
902: 
903:     email_sender
904:         .send_messages(&email_account, vec![message])
905:         .await?;
906: 
907:     Ok(())
908: }

910: fn create_prebooking_link(
911:     event_type: EventType,
912:     event_id: EventId,
913:     subscriber_id: i32,
914: ) -> Result<String> {
915:     let mut url = String::from("https://www.sv-eutingen.de/");
916:     url.push_str(match event_type {
917:         EventType::Fitness => "fitness",
918:         EventType::Events => "events",
919:     });
920:     url.push_str("?code=");
921: 
922:     // create the code
923:     url.push_str(&hashids::encode(&[
924:         event_id.into_inner().try_into()?,
925:         subscriber_id.try_into()?,
926:     ]));
927: 
928:     Ok(url)
929: }
930: 
931: fn read_payment_records(
932:     csv: &str,
933:     csv_start_date: Option<NaiveDate>,
934: ) -> Result<Vec<PaymentRecord>> {
935:     let mut records = Vec::new();
936: 
937:     for record in super::csv::read_payment_records(csv)? {
938:         // skip all records that are older than the start date
939:         if let Some(start_date) = csv_start_date
940:             && record.date < start_date
941:         {
942:             continue;
943:         }
944:         records.push(record);
945:     }
946: 
947:     Ok(records)
948: }

950: fn compare_payment_records_with_bookings(
951:     payment_records: &Vec<PaymentRecord>,
952:     bookings: &mut [VerifyPaymentBookingRecord],
953: ) -> Result<(HashMap<i32, String>, Vec<VerifyPaymentResult>)> {
954:     let mut verified_payment_bookings = Vec::new();
955:     let mut verified_ibans = HashMap::new();
956:     let mut payment_bookings_with_errors = BTreeMap::new();
957:     let mut non_matching_payment_records = Vec::new();
958: 
959:     let mut bookings = bookings
960:         .iter()
961:         .map(|booking| (&booking.payment_id, booking))
962:         .collect::<HashMap<_, _>>();
963: 
964:     for payment_record in payment_records {
965:         if payment_record.payment_ids.is_empty() {
966:             non_matching_payment_records.push(format!(
967:                 "{} / {} / {} / {}",
968:                 payment_record.payee,
969:                 payment_record.payee_iban,
970:                 payment_record.purpose,
971:                 payment_record.volumne.to_euro()
972:             ));
973:             continue;
974:         }
975: 
976:         // Try to match each payment_id individually
977:         let mut matched_bookings = Vec::new();
978:         let mut missing_ids = Vec::new();
979:         for payment_id in &payment_record.payment_ids {
980:             if let Some(booking) = bookings.remove(payment_id) {
981:                 matched_bookings.push((payment_id, booking));
982:             } else {
983:                 missing_ids.push(payment_id.to_owned());
984:             }
985:         }
986: 
987:         // Report missing IDs, but do not skip processing matched ones
988:         if !missing_ids.is_empty() {
989:             non_matching_payment_records.push(format!(
990:                 "Nicht erkannte Buchung(en): {} / {} / {} / {} / fehlende ID(s): {}",
991:                 payment_record.payee,
992:                 payment_record.payee_iban,
993:                 payment_record.purpose,
994:                 payment_record.volumne.to_euro(),
995:                 missing_ids.join(", ")
996:             ));
997:         }
998: 
999:         // Check for errors in individual bookings and collect valid ones
1000:         let mut error_ids = HashSet::new();
1001:         let mut error_total = false;
1002:         for (payment_id, booking) in &matched_bookings {
1003:             if booking.payment_confirmed_at.is_some() {
1004:                 payment_bookings_with_errors
1005:                     .entry(payment_id.to_owned())
1006:                     .or_insert_with(Vec::new)
1007:                     .push("Doppelt bezahlt: Buchung ist schon als bezahlt markiert".into());
1008:                 error_ids.insert(payment_id.to_owned());
1009:             }
1010:             if booking.enrolled && booking.canceled.is_some() {
1011:                 payment_bookings_with_errors
1012:                     .entry(payment_id.to_owned())
1013:                     .or_insert_with(Vec::new)
1014:                     .push("Falsch bezahlt: Buchung ist als storniert markiert".into());
1015:                 error_ids.insert(payment_id.to_owned());
1016:             }
1017:             if !booking.enrolled {
1018:                 payment_bookings_with_errors
1019:                     .entry(payment_id.to_owned())
1020:                     .or_insert_with(Vec::new)
1021:                     .push("Falsch bezahlt: Buchung ist von auf der Warteliste".into());
1022:                 error_ids.insert(payment_id.to_owned());
1023:             }
1024:         }
1025: 
1026:         // Check if sum of booking prices matches payment_record.volumne
1027:         let total_booking_price = matched_bookings
1028:             .iter()
1029:             .map(|(_, b)| b.price.clone())
1030:             .fold(bigdecimal::BigDecimal::from(0), |acc, x| acc + x);
1031:         let record_volumne = payment_record.volumne.clone();
1032:         if total_booking_price.clone() != record_volumne.clone() {
1033:             for (payment_id, _) in &matched_bookings {
1034:                 payment_bookings_with_errors
1035:                     .entry(payment_id.to_owned())
1036:                     .or_insert_with(Vec::new)
1037:                     .push(format!(
1038:                         "Betrag falsch: erwartet {} != überwiesen {}",
1039:                         total_booking_price.to_euro(),
1040:                         record_volumne.to_euro()
1041:                     ));
1042:                 error_ids.insert(payment_id.to_owned());
1043:             }
1044:             error_total = true;
1045:         }
1046: 
1047:         // Mark only correct bookings as verified
1048:         for (payment_id, booking) in matched_bookings {
1049:             if !error_ids.contains(payment_id) && !error_total {
1050:                 verified_payment_bookings.push(booking);
1051:                 verified_ibans.insert(booking.booking_id, payment_record.payee_iban.clone());
1052:             }
1053:         }
1054:     }
1055:     verified_payment_bookings.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.payment_id.cmp(&b.payment_id));
1056: 
1057:     let mut compare_result = Vec::new();
1058: 
1059:     // first group are the successfull matches
1060:     compare_result.push(VerifyPaymentResult::new(
1061:         format!(
1062:             "{} bezahlte {}",
1063:             verified_payment_bookings.len(),
1064:             match verified_payment_bookings.len() {
1065:                 1 => "Buchung",
1066:                 _ => "Buchungen",
1067:             }
1068:         ),
1069:         verified_payment_bookings
1070:             .iter()
1071:             .map(|booking| booking.payment_id.clone())
1072:             .collect::<Vec<_>>(),
1073:     ));
1074: 
1075:     // second group are the matches with errors
1076:     compare_result.push(VerifyPaymentResult::new(
1077:         format!(
1078:             "{} {} mit Problemen",
1079:             payment_bookings_with_errors.len(),
1080:             match payment_bookings_with_errors.len() {
1081:                 1 => "Buchung",
1082:                 _ => "Buchungen",
1083:             }
1084:         ),
1085:         payment_bookings_with_errors
1086:             .into_iter()
1087:             .map(|(payment_id, mut errors)| {
1088:                 errors.insert(0, payment_id.clone());
1089:                 errors.join(" / ")
1090:             })
1091:             .collect::<Vec<_>>(),
1092:     ));
1093: 
1094:     // third group are all non matching payment records
1095:     compare_result.push(VerifyPaymentResult::new(
1096:         format!(
1097:             "{} nicht erkannte {}",
1098:             non_matching_payment_records.len(),
1099:             match non_matching_payment_records.len() {
1100:                 1 => "Buchung",
1101:                 _ => "Buchungen",
1102:             }
1103:         ),
1104:         non_matching_payment_records,
1105:     ));
1106: 
1107:     Ok((verified_ibans, compare_result))
1108: }
1109: 
1110: /// Calculate the payday with the first event date.
1111: pub(super) fn calculate_payday(
1112:     booking_date: &DateTime<Utc>,
1113:     first_event_date: &DateTime<Utc>,
1114:     custom_days: Option<i64>,
1115: ) -> Result<NaiveDate> {
1116:     // default value is 14 days
1117:     let mut days = 14;
1118: 
1119:     // overwrite with custom days - if available
1120:     if let Some(custom_days) = custom_days {
1121:         days = custom_days;
1122:     }
1123: 
1124:     // calculated payday
1125:     let mut payday = first_event_date.date_naive()
1126:         - Duration::try_days(days)
1127:             .with_context(|| format!("Cannot create duration of {days} days."))?;
1128: 
1129:     // override the payday if it is before the day after the booking date
1130:     let earliest_paypay = booking_date.date_naive()
1131:         + Duration::try_days(1).with_context(|| "Cannot create duration of 1 day.")?;
1132:     if payday < earliest_paypay {
1133:         payday = earliest_paypay
1134:     }
1135: 
1136:     Ok(payday)
1137: }

1139: /// send participation confirmation after finished event
1140: pub(crate) async fn send_participation_confirmation(
1141:     pool: &PgPool,
1142:     event_id: EventId,
1143:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
1144: ) -> Result<usize> {
1145:     // fetch the event with all subscribers
1146:     let mut event = db::get_event(pool, &event_id, true)
1147:         .await?
1148:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Error fetching event with id '{}'", event_id.get_ref()))?;
1149:     let subscribers = event.subscribers.take().ok_or_else(|| {
1150:         anyhow!(
1151:             "Subscribers of event with id '{}' are missing",
1152:             event_id.get_ref()
1153:         )
1154:     })?;
1155: 
1156:     let template = match event.event_type {
1157:         EventType::Fitness => Ok(include_str!(
1158:             "../../templates/participation_confirmation_fitness.txt"
1159:         )),
1160:         EventType::Events => Err(anyhow!(
1161:             "Participation confirmation is not supported for event type 'Events'."
1162:         )),
1163:     }?;
1164: 
1165:     let dates_len = event.dates.len();
1166:     // abort if the event has no dates
1167:     if dates_len < 1 {
1168:         return Err(anyhow!(
1169:             "Participation confirmation is not supported for an event without dates."
1170:         ));
1171:     }
1172: 
1173:     let fmt = "%d. %B %Y";
1174:     let first_date = event
1175:         .dates
1176:         .first()
1177:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event with id '{}' has no first date", event_id))?
1178:         .format_localized(fmt, Locale::de_DE)
1179:         .to_string();
1180:     let last_date = event
1181:         .dates
1182:         .last()
1183:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event with id '{}' has no last date", event_id))?
1184:         .format_localized(fmt, Locale::de_DE)
1185:         .to_string();
1186:     let dates = format!("{dates_len} x {} Minuten", event.duration_in_minutes);
1187: 
1188:     // send an email per participant
1189:     let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
1190:     let subject = format!("{} Teilnahmebestätigung", event.subject_prefix());
1191:     let mut messages = Vec::new();
1192:     for subscriber in subscribers {
1193:         if subscriber.enrolled {
1194:             let price = subscriber.total_price(&event).to_euro();
1195: 
1196:             let bytes = export::create_participation_confirmation(
1197:                 subscriber.first_name.clone(),
1198:                 subscriber.last_name.clone(),
1199:                 event.name.clone(),
1200:                 first_date.clone(),
1201:                 last_date.clone(),
1202:                 price,
1203:                 dates.clone(),
1204:             )
1205:             .await?;
1206: 
1207:             let body = template::render_participation_confirmation(template, &event, &subscriber)?;
1208: 
1209:             let message = email_sender
1210:                 .build_message(&email_account)?
1211:                 .to(subscriber.email.parse()?)
1212:                 .subject(subject.clone())
1213:                 .multipart(
1214:                     MultiPart::mixed()
1215:                         .singlepart(SinglePart::plain(body))
1216:                         .singlepart(
1217:                             Attachment::new(String::from("Teilnahmebestätigung.pdf"))
1218:                                 .body(bytes, ContentType::parse("application/pdf")?),
1219:                         ),
1220:                 )?;
1221: 
1222:             messages.push(message)
1223:         }
1224:     }
1225: 
1226:     let count = messages.len();
1227:     if count > 0 {
1228:         email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
1229:     }
1230: 
1231:     Ok(count)
1232: }

parts.text:
I have a good understanding of events.rs now. Let me read the rest of the file (tests section) and the EmailGateway seam from #71. Let me also look at the email module, test_utils, and api.rs to understand the EmailGateway mock.

Let me categorize the functions in events.rs into notification vs non-notification.

From the issue:
- "Extract **only** the notification fan-out: the ~4 functions that build notification emails and the templates they use."
- "Booking lifecycle, payment operations, and event-query functions stay where they are."
- "`events::notifications` takes `&impl EmailGateway` directly (the seam from #71) and stops being a sibling of the rest of `events.rs` for the purpose of email access."

So notification fan-out functions. Let me look at the public functions that send emails but are NOT about booking lifecycle/payment/events queries:

Functions in events.rs (public `pub(crate)` ones):
1. `get_events` (line 33) - event query - STAYS
2. `get_all_custom_fields` (line 54) - event query - STAYS
3. `get_event_counters` (line 61) - event query - STAYS
4. `booking` (line 65) - booking lifecycle - STAYS
5. `prebook_with_iban` (line 83) - booking lifecycle - STAYS
6. `prebooking` (line 93) - booking lifecycle - STAYS
7. `update` (line 107) - sends "schedule change" emails - this is event lifecycle? Hmm. Actually update is event lifecycle (write_event). The email is a notification fan-out about schedule change.
8. `delete` (line 149) - event lifecycle - STAYS
9. `verify_payments` (line 153) - payment operation - STAYS
10. `get_unpaid_bookings` (line 182) - event query/payment - STAYS
11. `update_payment` (line 243) - payment operation - STAYS
12. `export_sepa_xml` (line 251) - payment operation - STAYS
13. `cancel_booking` (line 328) - booking lifecycle - STAYS (sends email within booking lifecycle)
14. `send_event_email` (line 382) - NOTIFICATION - MOVE (sends event email to bookings/waiting list)
15. `send_event_reminders` (line 461) - NOTIFICATION - MOVE (sends reminder emails before event start)
16. `send_payment_reminders` (line 520) - NOTIFICATION - MOVE (sends payment reminder emails)
17. `close_finished_running_events` (line 592) - this is lifecycle-ish and calls `send_participation_confirmation`. Hmm.
18. `send_participation_confirmation` (line 1140) - NOTIFICATION - MOVE (sends participation confirmation after finished event)

Hmm, so the 4 notification functions are likely:
- `send_event_email`
- `send_event_reminders`
- `send_payment_reminders`
- `send_participation_confirmation`

That's exactly 4! These are the "~4 functions that build notification emails".

`cancel_booking` sends an email but as part of the booking lifecycle (cancellation + waiting list promotion), so it stays. Same with `update` (schedule change on event update) — but wait, does `update` count as notification? It's "event lifecycle" — it writes the event and sends "schedule change" emails. The issue says "Booking lifecycle, payment operations, and event-query functions stay where they are." `update` is event lifecycle data write that also fans out emails. Hmm.

Let me think about what the "notification fan-out" means. The pure notification fan-out functions are the ones whose primary purpose is to send notifications (not the booking ones which incidentally send confirmation emails as part of the lifecycle).

The clear candidates: `send_event_email`, `send_event_reminders`, `send_payment_reminders`, `send_participation_confirmation`. These have `send_` prefix and pure notification role. That's exactly 4.

`close_finished_running_events` calls `send_participation_confirmation` for fitness events - it's a cron/job lifecycle coordinator. It stays in the main module but will call into `events::notifications::send_participation_confirmation`.

`update` has inline schedule-change email fan-out. Hmm, but this is triggered by `close_finished_running_events` via `update()`. Wait — `close_finished_running_events` calls `update()` which has its own email fan-out logic for lifecycle status changes? Actually no. Let me re-read.

`update` (line 107): writes event, if dates were removed and event is review/published/running, fan-out schedule change emails to bookings. This is "event lifecycle write" with embedded schedule-change notification.

Hmm, the issue says "Booking lifecycle, payment operations, and event-query functions stay where they are." It doesn't explicitly mention event-lifecycle. But the 4 functions are clearly the `send_*` ones.

Wait — does `update` count as a notification function? Its primary purpose is writing the event (event lifecycle), with schedule-change email as a side effect. It's ambiguous. The issue says "the ~4 functions that build notification emails and the templates they use."

Let me count "templates they use":
- `send_event_email` uses `data.body` (dynamic template passed in API)
- `send_event_reminders` uses `event_reminder_fitness.txt` / `event_reminder_events.txt`
- `send_payment_reminders` uses `payment_reminder_fitness.txt` / `payment_reminder_events.txt`
- `send_participation_confirmation` uses `participation_confirmation_fitness.txt`

So these 4 own templates: event_reminder_*, payment_reminder_*, participation_confirmation_*. That's a clear set of notification templates.

`update` uses `schedule_change_fitness.txt` / `schedule_change_events.txt` — these are schedule change templates. So `update` does use templates too. Hmm.

But `update` is not "fan-out" in the same sense — it's a write operation. And the issue says exactly 4 functions move. The 4 `send_*` functions are the clear set. Let me go with those 4:
- `send_event_email`
- `send_event_reminders`
- `send_payment_reminders`
- `send_participation_confirmation`

Now, dependencies these 4 have on the rest of events.rs:
- `send_event_email` uses `create_prebooking_link` (a private helper in events.rs, line 910). Hmm! `create_prebooking_link` uses `hashids`. This function is also... is it used elsewhere? Let me check. It's defined privately. Used in `send_event_email`.
- `send_event_reminders` uses `db`, `template`, `Email::new`, `EmailGateway`.
- `send_payment_reminders` uses `get_unpaid_bookings` (a public function in events.rs!) — so notifications depends on `get_unpaid_bookings`. This is a problem because `events::notifications` shouldn't reach across to the rest of events.rs.

Wait — the issue says "events::notifications takes &impl EmailGateway directly ... and stops being a sibling of the rest of events.rs for the purpose of email access." That's specifically about EMAIL access, not about everything. But it also says "stops being a sibling of the rest of events.rs for the purpose of email access." So reaching across for non-email helpers (like `get_unpaid_bookings`) is still... hmm.

Let me re-read acceptance criteria:
- "events::notifications accepts &impl EmailGateway and no longer reaches across to the rest of events.rs for email access"

So it's specifically about email access. Reaching across for `get_unpaid_bookings` may be OK? But `get_unpaid_bookings` is `pub(crate)` in events.rs. If notifications is a submodule of events (`events::notifications`), it can access `super::get_unpaid_bookings` (if it's `pub(crate)` or `pub(super)`).

But the spirit of "deep module" / vertical slice suggests we want to minimize cross-references. Let me check what dependencies each of the 4 has:

1. `send_event_email`:
   - `pool: &PgPool`, `data: EventEmail`, `email_sender`
   - `db::get_bookings`, `db::get_event`
   - `create_prebooking_link` (private helper in events.rs) — uses `hashids`, `EventType`, `EventId`
   - `template::render_booking`
   - `Email::new`, `.into_message`
   - `event.get_associated_email_account`
   
2. `send_event_reminders`:
   - `db::get_reminder_events`, `db::mark_as_reminder_sent`
   - `template::render_event_reminder`
   - `event_reminder_*.txt`
   - `Email::new`, `.into_message`

3. `send_payment_reminders`:
   - `get_unpaid_bookings` (events.rs public) — the key cross-referenced function
   - `db::get_event`, `db::mark_as_payment_reminder_sent`
   - `template::render_payment_reminder`
   - `payment_reminder_*.txt`
   - `email_sender.account AccountLookup::ByType` — needs EmailGateway account lookup
   - `Email::new`, `.into_message`

4. `send_participation_confirmation`:
   - `db::get_event`
   - `export::create_participation_confirmation`
   - `template::render_participation_confirmation`
   - `participation_confirmation_fitness.txt`
   - `event.get_associated_email_account`
   - `EmailGateway` message building, multipart, attachment

So dependencies crossing into rest-of-events:
- `create_prebooking_link` (used by send_event_email) — private helper. This could move to notifications OR stay and be called via super. Since it's about building a prebooking link for an email, it makes sense to move it to notifications. But it's also used elsewhere? Let me grep.
- `get_unpaid_bookings` (used by send_payment_reminders) — public function, used to
continue
Let me continue reading the rest of events.rs to understand the full file, and look at the email gateway structure.

1610:         let payment_records = read_payment_records(csv, csv_start_date).unwrap();
1611:         compare_payment_records_with_bookings(&payment_records, bookings).unwrap()
1612:     }

1614:     #[test]
1615:     fn test_calculate_payday() {
1616:         let booking_date = Utc::now();
1617: 
1618:         // event starts in 3 weeks
1619:         let start_date = Utc::now() + Duration::try_weeks(3).unwrap();
1620:         assert_eq!(
1621:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, None).unwrap(),
1622:             Utc::now().date_naive() + Duration::try_weeks(1).unwrap()
1623:         );
1624:         assert_eq!(
1625:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(7)).unwrap(),
1626:             Utc::now().date_naive() + Duration::try_weeks(2).unwrap()
1627:         );
1628:         assert_eq!(
1629:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(0)).unwrap(),
1630:             Utc::now().date_naive() + Duration::try_weeks(3).unwrap()
1631:         );
1632:         let tomorrow = Utc::now().date_naive() + Duration::try_days(1).unwrap();
1633:         assert_eq!(
1634:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(21)).unwrap(),
1635:             tomorrow
1636:         );
1637:         assert_eq!(
1638:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(28)).unwrap(),
1639:             tomorrow
1640:         );
1641: 
1642:         // event starts in 3 days
1643:         let start_date = Utc::now() + Duration::try_days(3).unwrap();
1644:         assert_eq!(
1645:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(1)).unwrap(),
1646:             Utc::now().date_naive() + Duration::try_days(2).unwrap()
1647:         );
1648:         assert_eq!(
1649:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(2)).unwrap(),
1650:             tomorrow
1651:         );
1652:         assert_eq!(
1653:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(3)).unwrap(),
1654:             tomorrow
1655:         );
1656:         assert_eq!(
1657:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(14)).unwrap(),
1658:             tomorrow
1659:         );
1660: 
1661:         // event starts today
1662:         let start_date = Utc::now();
1663:         assert_eq!(
1664:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, None).unwrap(),
1665:             tomorrow
1666:         );
1667:         assert_eq!(
1668:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(7)).unwrap(),
1669:             tomorrow
1670:         );
1671: 
1672:         // event started yesterday
1673:         let start_date = Utc::now() - Duration::try_days(1).unwrap();
1674:         assert_eq!(
1675:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, None).unwrap(),
1676:             tomorrow
1677:         );
1678:         assert_eq!(
1679:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(7)).unwrap(),
1680:             tomorrow
1681:         );
1682: 
1683:         // negative paydays (because the bookings are in the past)
1684:         let booking_date = Utc::now() - Duration::try_days(31).unwrap();
1685:         let start_date = Utc::now();
1686:         assert_eq!(
1687:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, None).unwrap(),
1688:             Utc::now().date_naive() - Duration::try_days(14).unwrap()
1689:         );
1690:         assert_eq!(
1691:             calculate_payday(&booking_date, &start_date, Some(7)).unwrap(),
1692:             Utc::now().date_naive() - Duration::try_days(7).unwrap()
1693:         );
1694:     }

1696:     #[test]
1697:     fn test_calc_due_in_days() {
1698:         // without custom days
1699:         let booking_date = Utc::now();
1700:         let start_date = Utc::now() + Duration::try_days(28).unwrap();
1701:         assert_eq!(
1702:             calc_due_in_days(booking_date, start_date, String::from("")).unwrap(),
1703:             14
1704:         );
1705: 
1706:         let booking_date = Utc::now();
1707:         let start_date = Utc::now() + Duration::try_days(14).unwrap();
1708:         assert_eq!(
1709:             calc_due_in_days(booking_date, start_date, String::from("")).unwrap(),
1710:             1
1711:         );
1712: 
1713:         let booking_date = Utc::now() - Duration::try_days(4).unwrap();
1714:         let start_date = Utc::now() + Duration::try_days(7).unwrap();
1715:         assert_eq!(
1716:             calc_due_in_days(booking_date, start_date, String::from("{{payday}}")).unwrap(),
1717:             -3
1718:         );
1719: 
1720:         // with custom days
1721:         let booking_date = Utc::now();
1722:         let start_date = Utc::now() + Duration::try_days(7).unwrap();
1723:         assert_eq!(
1724:             calc_due_in_days(booking_date, start_date, String::from("{{payday 7}}")).unwrap(),
1725:             1
1726:         );
1727:         let booking_date = Utc::now() - Duration::try_days(31).unwrap();
1728:         let start_date = Utc::now() + Duration::try_days(7).unwrap();
1729:         assert_eq!(
1730:             calc_due_in_days(booking_date, start_date, String::from("{{payday 1}}")).unwrap(),
1731:             6
1732:         );
1733:         assert_eq!(
1734:             calc_due_in_days(booking_date, start_date, String::from("{{payday 14}}")).unwrap(),
1735:             -7
1736:         );
1737:     }

1754:     /// Mock gateway where account-by-type lookup is expected exactly `times`
1755:     /// and sends are discarded. Used by tests that assert an email fires a
1756:     /// bounded number of times without inspecting content.
1757:     fn mock_gateway_discarding(
1758:         accounts: Vec<(crate::models::EmailType, &str)>,
1759:     ) -> crate::email::MockEmailGateway {
1760:         mock_email_gateway(accounts).0
1761:     }
1762: 
1763:     fn mock_gateway_times(
1764:         accounts: Vec<(crate::models::EmailType, &str)>,
1765:         times: usize,
1766:     ) -> crate::email::MockEmailGateway {
1767:         use crate::email::{AccountLookup, MockEmailGateway};
1768:         use crate::models::EmailAccount;
1769:         let mut mock = MockEmailGateway::new();
1770:         for (email_type, address) in accounts {
1771:             let account = EmailAccount::new_for_test(email_type.clone(), address);
1772:             let by_type = email_type.clone();
1773:             mock.expect_account()
1774:                 .withf(move |lookup| matches!(lookup, AccountLookup::ByType(t) if t == &by_type))
1775:                 .times(times)
1776:                 .returning(move |_| {
1777:                     let account = account.clone();
1778:                     Box::pin(async move { Ok(account) })
1779:                 });
1780:         }
1781:         mock.expect_build_message().returning(|account| {
1782:             Ok(lettre::Message::builder()
1783:                 .from(account.mailbox()?)
1784:                 .date_now())
1785:         });
1786:         mock.expect_send_messages()
1787:             .returning(|_, _| Box::pin(async { Ok(()) }));
1788:         mock
1789:     }

1: use std::str::from_utf8;
2: use std::sync::Arc;
3: 
4: use anyhow::{Context, Result, bail};
5: use async_trait::async_trait;
6: use lettre::message::Mailbox;
7: use lettre::message::MessageBuilder;
8: use lettre::transport::smtp::authentication::Credentials;
9: use lettre::{AsyncSmtpTransport, AsyncTransport, Message, Tokio1Executor};
10: #[cfg(test)]
11: use mockall::automock;
12: 
13: use crate::{
14:     logic::secrets::{SecretKey, SecretProvider},
15:     models::{EmailAccount, EmailType},
16: };
17: 
18: /// How to look up an [`EmailAccount`] on the [`EmailGateway`].
19: ///
20: /// Unifies the two pre-existing lookup modes (`get_account_by_type` and
21: /// `get_account_by_address`) behind a single gateway method so the trait stays
22: /// shallow while still supporting events that override their sender via
23: /// `alt_email_address`.
24: #[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
25: pub(crate) enum AccountLookup {
26:     ByType(EmailType),
27:     ByAddress(String),
28: }
29: 
30: impl From<EmailType> for AccountLookup {
31:     fn from(email_type: EmailType) -> Self {
32:         Self::ByType(email_type)
33:     }
34: }
35: 
36: fn mailbox(account: &EmailAccount) -> Result<Mailbox> {
37:     Ok(account.address.parse()?)
38: }
39: 
40: fn create_mailer(account: &EmailAccount) -> Result<AsyncSmtpTransport<Tokio1Executor>> {
41:     let transport = AsyncSmtpTransport::<Tokio1Executor>::relay("smtp.gmail.com")?
42:         .credentials(Credentials::new(
43:             account.address.clone(),
44:             from_utf8(&account.password)
45:                 .with_context(|| {
46:                     format!("Invalid UTF-8 sequence in password of {}", account.address)
47:                 })?
48:                 .into(),
49:         ))
50:         .build();
51:     Ok(transport)
52: }
53: 
54: async fn send_messages(from: &EmailAccount, messages: Vec<Message>) -> Result<()> {
55:     let mailer = create_mailer(from)?;
56:     for message in messages {
57:         mailer.send(message).await?;
58:     }
59:     Ok(())
60: }
61: 
62: /// Deep email seam: every outbound email in the codebase goes through this
63: /// trait. It owns the three operations a caller needs — sending a batch of
64: /// pre-built messages, building a message rooted at a sender account, and
65: /// resolving the sender account itself — so that `new_message_builder` and
66: /// `mailbox` can stay private to this module.
67: #[async_trait]
68: #[cfg_attr(test, automock)]
69: pub trait EmailGateway {
70:     /// Send `messages` from `from`, batching over a single SMTP connection.
71:     async fn send_messages(&self, from: &EmailAccount, messages: Vec<Message>) -> Result<()>;
72: 
73:     /// Start building a [`Message`] rooted at `account` (sets `from` and the
74:     /// current date). Callers chain `.to()`, `.subject()`, `.singlepart()`,
75:     /// etc. and finalize with `?` on the resulting `Message`.
76:     fn build_message(&self, account: &EmailAccount) -> Result<MessageBuilder>;
77: 
78:     /// Resolve the [`EmailAccount`] for a given lookup (by type or by
79:     /// explicit address, e.g. an event's `alt_email_address`).
80:     async fn account(&self, lookup: AccountLookup) -> Result<EmailAccount>;
81: }
82: 
83: #[derive(Clone)]
84: pub(crate) struct RealEmailGateway {
85:     secrets: Arc<dyn SecretProvider>,
86: }
87: 
88: impl RealEmailGateway {
89:     pub(crate) fn new(secrets: Arc<dyn SecretProvider>) -> Self {
90:         Self { secrets }
91:     }
92: 
93:     async fn email_accounts(&self) -> Result<Vec<EmailAccount>> {
94:         let email_accounts: Vec<EmailAccount> =
95:             serde_json::from_str(&self.secrets.get(SecretKey::EmailAccounts).await?)?;
96:         Ok(email_accounts)
97:     }
98: 
99:     /// Test the SMTP connectivity of every configured account. Health-check
100:     /// only — not part of [`EmailGateway`] because it has no business logic
101:     /// and never needs mocking beyond the connectivity task wrapper.
102:     pub(crate) async fn test_connection(&self) -> Result<()> {
103:         let mut errors = Vec::new();
104:         for email_account in self.email_accounts().await? {
105:             let result = create_mailer(&email_account)?.test_connection().await;
106:             match result {
107:                 Ok(result) => {
108:                     if !result {
109:                         errors.push(format!(
110:                             "Testing connection of {} failed: {}",
111:                             email_account.address, "test_connection returned false"
112:                         ))
113:                     }
114:                 }
115:                 Err(e) => errors.push(format!(
116:                     "Testing connection of {} failed: {}",
117:                     email_account.address, e
118:                 )),
119:             }
120:         }
121: 
122:         if !errors.is_empty() {
123:             bail!(
124:                 "{} errors while testing connections:\n\n{}",
125:                 errors.len(),
126:                 errors.join("\n")
127:             );
128:         }
129: 
130:         Ok(())
131:     }
132: }
133: 
134: #[async_trait]
135: impl EmailGateway for RealEmailGateway {
136:     async fn send_messages(&self, from: &EmailAccount, messages: Vec<Message>) -> Result<()> {
137:         send_messages(from, messages).await
138:     }
139: 
140:     fn build_message(&self, account: &EmailAccount) -> Result<MessageBuilder> {
141:         Ok(Message::builder().from(mailbox(account)?).date_now())
142:     }
143: 
144:     async fn account(&self, lookup: AccountLookup) -> Result<EmailAccount> {
145:         let accounts = self.email_accounts().await?;
146:         match lookup {
147:             AccountLookup::ByType(email_type) => accounts
148:                 .into_iter()
149:                 .find(|account| account.email_type == email_type)
150:                 .with_context(|| format!("Found no email account for email type {:?}", email_type)),
151:             AccountLookup::ByAddress(email_address) => accounts
152:                 .into_iter()
153:                 .find(|account| account.address == email_address)
154:                 .with_context(|| {
155:                     format!(
156:                         "Found no email account for email address '{}'",
157:                         email_address
158:                     )
159:                 }),
160:         }
161:     }
162: }

1: use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
2: 
3: use lettre::Message;
4: 
5: use crate::email::{AccountLookup, MockEmailGateway};
6: use crate::models::{EmailAccount, EmailType};
7: 
8: /// Canonical mock [`EmailGateway`]. Configures every lookup mode and a
9: /// capturing `send_messages` so each test gets:
10: ///   - account resolution (by type *and* by address, matching the `accounts`
11: ///     list, with the rest returning the configured address)
12: ///   - a real `MessageBuilder` from `build_message` so messages build offline
13: ///   - `send_messages` captures `(account, Vec<Message>)` per call
14: ///
15: /// Tests that don't care about captured messages simply ignore the returned
16: /// buffer. No-op cases (no expectations needed) use `MockEmailGateway::new()`

- calls `email_sender.send_messages(...)`

parts.text:
Clean clippy baseline. Let me now state my seams and the move plan clearly.

**Seams under test (pre-agreed per issue #77):**

2. Notification tests run offline via the `EmailGateway` mock (`MockEmailGateway` / `mock_email_gateway`) — no real SMTP.
3. DB via `#[sqlx::test]` is fine (it's a locally-managed test DB).

**Code that moves to `events/notifications.rs`:**
- `send_event_email` (lines 382-458)
- `send_event_reminders` (461-517)
- `send_payment_reminders` (520-588)
- `send_participation_confirmation` (1140-1232)

- `test_send_participation_confirmation_events_type`
- `test_send_participation_confirmation_no_dates`
- `test_send_payment_reminders`
(in `events_integration_tests`)

**Tests that stay in `mod.rs`:**
- `test_compare_csv_with_bookings`, `test_calculate_payday`, `test_calc_due_in_days` (in `mod tests`)
- All booking/SEPA/lifecycle tests (in `events_integration_tests`)

**Shared test helpers going to `test_utils.rs` as `pub(crate)`:**
- `mock_gateway_discarding`
- `mock_gateway_times`

These need to be `pub(crate)` so both `mod.rs`'s test mod and `notifications.rs`'s test mod can use them. Move them to test_utils.rs (already has `mock_email_gateway`).

**Path adjustments:**
- `include_str!("../../templates/...")` → `include_str!("../../../templates/...")` (file moved one dir deeper)
- `super::{banking, export, template}` → in mod.rs stays as-is. In `notifications.rs` template/etc via `crate::logic::{export, template}` or `super::super::template`.
- `super::csv` (remains in mod.rs; csv still in `crate::logic::csv` mod). mod.rs unchanged.
- `super::news` (used by subscribe_to_updates only — stays in mod.rs). Unchanged.
- mod.rs's `close_finished_running_events` calls `send_participation_confirmation(...)`. → `notifications::send_participation_confirmation(...)`.
- `send_payment_reminders` calls `get_unpaid_bookings` (stays in mod.rs). From notifications.rs: `super::get_unpaid_bookings`.

**call sites updated:**
- `api.rs:849` `events::send_event_email` → `events::notifications::send_event_email`
- `tasks.rs:30` `events::send_event_reminders` → `events::notifications::send_event_reminders`
- `tasks.rs:52` `events::send_payment_reminders` → `events::notifications::send_payment_reminders`
- `tasks.rs:71` `events::send_participation_confirmation` → `events::notifications::send_participation_confirmation`

Now the question for visibility: `events/notifications.rs` functions need to be `pub(crate)`. They will be accessed from `tasks.rs` (which is `crate::logic::tasks`) via `events::notifications::*` → that requires `events::notifications` mod to be `pub(crate)` (or pub use). The `events` mod has `pub(crate) mod events;` in `logic/mod.rs`. Within events/mod.rs, declare `pub(crate) mod notifications;`. Functions in notifications.rs publish as `pub(crate)`. Then access `crate::logic::events::notifications::send_event_reminders`.

For `close_finished_running_events` (in mod.rs) calling `notifications::send_participation_confirmation` — mod.rs declares `mod notifications;` as `pub(crate) mod notifications;` — that makes it visible within mod.rs too (no need for `pub(crate)` from mod.rs's own perspective, but doesn't hurt). Actually within mod.rs, just `mod notifications;` would suffice and limit visibility to `events` mod. But callers (tasks.rs) need to reach it as `events::notifications`. Since `events` is `pub(crate)` and visible to `crate::logic::tasks` via `super::events` (tasks' super is `crate::logic`), then `events::notifications::send_event_reminders` requires `notifications` to be visible — needs `pub(crate)` on the mod declaration. Without `pub(crate)`, the module would be private to `events`, and `events::notifications` would fail from `tasks.rs`.

So: `pub(crate) mod notifications;` in mod.rs. Functions inside `pub(crate)`.

- send_payment_reminders: no  -- the old version had `let key = booking.event_id;`. No with_context.
- send_participation_confirmation: no
- create_prebooking_link: uses `try_into()?` and `try_from`? Let me look at lines 910-929.

```rust
fn create_prebooking_link(
    event_type: EventType,
    event_id: EventId,
    subscriber_id: i32,
) -> Result<String> {
    let mut url = String::from("https://www.sv-eutingen.de/");
    url.push_str(match event_type {
        EventType::Fitness => "fitness",
        EventType::Events => "events",
    });
    url.push_str("?code=");
    url.push_str(&hashids::encode(&[
        event_id.into_inner().try_into()?,
        subscriber_id.try_into()?,
    ]));
    Ok(url)
}
```

`try_into()?` — needs `Result` and `TryInto`. The `?` works with anyhow::Result since the conversion errors convert to anyhow::Error. No `Context` needed. So `Context` is not strictly needed.

Actually wait — is `Context` used anywhere? Let me carefully grep each function. Looking at the snippets:
- send_payment_reminders line 528-530: uses `.into_iter().filter(|booking| matches!(booking.due_in_days, Some(due_in_days) if due_in_days < 0))`. No Context.
- send_participation_confirmation: format!, `?` operators.

I'll drop Context. If clippy complains, add it back. Actually building will tell me.

`bail!` is used by send_event_email line 388:
```rust

}
```

So `bail!` needed. `anyhow!` used by:

Now let me also check the test helpers I move. The moved unit test mod `mod tests` in events.rs has 4 tests:
